reconciliation.scope: Release arc: v0.14.0 (derived on read from the last reachable tag) through evaluated_commit (intended release v0.15.0). The handshake SHAs (evaluated_commit / evaluated_tree / manifest_parent_commit) bind to R' (the readiness-artifact commit re-pinned to release-prep base 28deee3), which sits between the pre-existing note commit (07d37f3, resumable_existing_note — the note is in history, not re-authored) and M' (this manifest commit) in the wrapper-required three-commit ceremony note(N) -> artifact(R') -> manifest(M'). At HEAD=M' the DEFER handshake invariant requires HEAD^ == R' == evaluated_commit == manifest_parent_commit AND tree(HEAD^) == evaluated_tree == tree(R'); the readiness gate additionally requires the committed artifact (.vh-agent-harness/release-readiness-pass.json) to carry commit_sha == HEAD^^ == 28deee3 (the release-prep base under resumable_existing_note) and to be the single path changed in HEAD^^..HEAD^. Release mode is manifest-authority-only; the wrapper and CI recheck always read this committed manifest. release_base.value is advisory (derive-on-read via gitLatestTag); kind stays operator-attested. records[] dispositions are operator-attested and unchanged by the releaser's SHA recompute.

records.summary: P1-LINEAGE-002 deferred coverage (F6/F7) — conditional on a future prune phase.
records.reason: Not part of the current release. Retained for audit with unsupported legacy trigger metadata (the source card's trigger grammar uses a '||' separator, a directory operand, and a non-predicate feature: term, which the strict release-mode predicate parser rejects). Re-evaluate only when a prune phase is pursued or renderstate/seam is touched for deletion logic. The canonical no+disclose+invalid disposition lets the release proceed while disclosing the conditional follow-up and its invalid metadata state.
